# Mako Programming Language

> Source: https://mako-lang.com
> Full reference for LLMs: https://mako-lang.com/llms-full.txt
> Repository: https://github.com/loreste/mako

## What is Mako?

Mako is a compiled, statically typed programming language. Source files use the
`.mko` extension. **Unique language, unique syntax** (Mako flair: `fn`, `let`,
`on`, `pack`, `pull`, `hold`, `share`, `arena`, `crew`, `kick`). Exists to fix
Go/Rust *pain* (GC/nil/err noise/leaks; lifetimes/traits/async coloring) without
cloning either — docs/PAIN_POINTS.md. Dual spellings are compat sugar only.
Identity: docs/IDENTITY.md (~90%). Compiles to C then native via clang. No GC.

Key properties:
- Mako surface: fn, let, on Type, pack/pull, hold/share/arena, crew/kick/join, match, export
- Low ceremony: infer locals, print(any common), string ==, ?, match routes — docs/ERGONOMICS.md
- Units: always pack-qualified; preferred `pull "p" as n` (dual: import)
- Dual sugar still parses: func, :=, var, package, import, type T struct, receivers
- Speed is the game: as close to Rust as possible (no GC; -O3 -flto; docs/SPEED.md)
- Concurrency first-class: crew/kick/join, channels, select, actor (structured)
- Parallelism first-class: fan + multi-kick crews
- Compiles .mko -> C -> native binary (debug -O0, release -O3 -flto)
- No GC: ownership via hold/share, arenas for scoped allocation
- Result/Option types enforced (no null, unused Result is a compile error)
- Batteries-included stdlib: HTTP server, JSON, SQL, crypto, regex, UUID, etc.
- WASM/WASI target support (wasm32-wasip1)

## Installation

```bash
curl -fsSL https://github.com/loreste/mako/releases/latest/download/install-release.sh | bash
```

Or from source:
```bash
make install    # installs to ~/.local/bin/mako
```

## Quick Start

```bash
mako init hello --name hello
cd hello
mako run main.mko
mako build main.mko
```

## Hello World (preferred Mako syntax)

```mko
fn main() {
    print("hello from mako")
    print_int(fib(10))
}

fn fib(n: int) -> int {
    if n <= 1 {
        return n
    }
    return fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2)
}
```

Identity: docs/IDENTITY.md. Dual sugar: func, :=, a int still parse.

## Core Concepts

- **Types:** int, int8, int32, int64, uint64, float, bool, string, byte, void
- **Composite:** []T (slices), map[K]V, chan[T], Option[T], Result[T, E], (T, U) tuples
- **Declarations:** fn, struct, enum, actor, interface, const, export (preferred)
- **Methods:** on T { fn M(self) … } preferred; T_M free functions; dual Go receivers
- **Locals:** let / let mut preferred; dual := / var
- **Control flow:** if/else, while, for/in/range, match, break, continue, return, defer
- **Ownership:** hold (move), share (RC), arena (bump allocator)
- **Concurrency:** crew { t.kick(f()); j.join() }, fan, channels, select
- **Error handling:** Result[T, E], Ok/Err, error("msg"), ?, multi-return
- **Units:** `pack name`; `pull "path"` / `pull "path" as name` / `pull ( … )`; always qualify (`pkg.fn`); dual `import`/`package`; specialized `_` / `.`
- **Testing:** fn TestFoo() { assert_eq(1, 1) } in *_test.mko files

## Build Commands

```bash
mako build file.mko              # debug build
mako build --release file.mko    # optimized -O3 -flto
mako run file.mko                # compile and run
mako test path/                  # run tests
mako fmt file.mko -w             # format in place
mako check file.mko              # typecheck only
mako build --target wasm32-wasip1 file.mko -o out.wasm
```

## Stdlib Categories

Output, Strings, Math, File I/O, HTTP Server/Client, JSON, Database (SQLite/
Postgres), Crypto (SHA, HMAC, AES-GCM, ChaCha20), Encoding (base64, hex, gzip),
Regex, UUID, Cookies/Sessions/Auth, Channels, Concurrency (mutex, atomics, CMap),
Event Loop, Binary Buffer, Game Networking, Cloud (consistent hashing, rate
limiting, circuit breaker), Testing.

## Documentation Links

- Language Guide: https://mako-lang.com/docs/guide
- Standard Library: https://mako-lang.com/docs/stdlib
- The Mako Book: https://mako-lang.com/docs/book
- Builtins Reference: https://mako-lang.com/docs/builtins
- Keywords: https://mako-lang.com/docs/keywords
- Memory Safety: https://mako-lang.com/docs/security
- Build System: https://mako-lang.com/docs/build
- WASM/WASI: https://mako-lang.com/docs/wasm
- Full LLM Reference: https://mako-lang.com/llms-full.txt
